items.content:
Create New Device Profile :-
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Profiles" section. Then go to the "Device Profile" page.
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new Device Profile".

Create New Device Profile :-
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Profiles" section. Then go to the "Device Profile" page. 11
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new Device Profile".

Device Profile Details:
Rule chain -
By default, the Root Rule Chain processes all incoming messages and events for any device. However, the more different device types you have, the more complex your Root Rule Chain may become. Many platform users create their Root Rule Chain for the sole purpose of sending messages to specific rule chains depending on the device type.
Queue Name -
• Enter the details. Click "Add" to add the device profile.
• Your first device profile has been added. As new device profile are added, they will be added to the top of the table, since the table sort assets using the time of the creation by default.

Transport Configuration :
The current version of the Optima platform supports the following transport types: Default, MQTT, CoAP

Alarm Rule -
Platform users can use Rule Engine to configure alarms.
Device Provision -

Edit Device Profile -
  • Clicking the device profile row to open its details, then click the "pencil" icon in the upper right corner of the dialog.
• If necessary, change the device profile name and any things. After editing, click the orange check mark to save all applied changes.

Delete Device Profile -
  • Click on the “trash can” icon can opposite the device profile name you want to delete.

Create New Device :-
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Entities" section. Then go to the "Devices" page.
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new device".
  • Enter the device name. For example, "My New Device". Click "Add" to add the device.
• Your first device has been added. As new devices are added, they will be added to the top of the table, since the table sort devices using the time of the creation by default.

• When adding a new device, you will receive a notification. You can view it by clicking on the “bell” icon in the top right corner.

Edit Device -
  • Clicking the device row to open its details, then click the "pencil" icon in the upper right corner of the dialog.
• If necessary, change the device name and its profile. Also, you can input label and description. After editing, click the orange check mark to save all applied changes.

Delete Device -
  • Click on the “trash can” icon can opposite the device's name you want to delete.
  • Confirm deleting the device in the dialog box.

Device operations :

Copy device Id

  • Copy the device Id to the clipboard using the “Copy Device Id” button.

Copy device access

  • Click on the “Copy Access Token”  button to copy the device credentials.

Device details -
The “Device details” window provides various tabs that allow you to manage and monitor information such as attributes, telemetry, alarm, events ,relation configuration & version control of the device.
1. Device attributes
This tab displays the client, server, and shared attributes of the device. For example, serial number, model, and firmware version. Attributes are static and semi-static key-value pairs associated with devices.

2.Device telemetry
This tab shows the real-time telemetry data the device sends, such as sensor readings, status, and other measurable variables. Time-Series data points are available for storage, querying, and visualization. For example, temperature, humidity, and battery level.

3.Device alarms

This tab shows alarms that identify issues with your devices.

4.Device events

Here, events related to the device are displayed, including system logs, errors, warnings, and other important moments in the device’s lifecycle.

Device relations

Relation are directed connections to other entities. This tab displays the relationships of this device with other devices, dashboards, assets, and other entities in the Optima system.

Version control

Optima version control service provides the ability to export and restore Optima Entities using Git.

Create New Dashboard -
A dashboard in Optima allows users to visualize and monitor data collected from IoT devices.
Create an empty dashboard -
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Dashboards" page through the main menu on the left of the screen. Then, click the "+" sign in the upper right corner of the screen, and select "Create new dashboard" from the drop-down menu.
• In the opened dialog, it is necessary to enter a dashboard title, description is optional. Click "Add".
• Your first dashboard has been successfully created. As you continue to add new dashboards, they will appear at the top of the list. This default sorting is based on the creation timestamp.

Add an Entities table widget -
The “Entities table” widget displays a list of entities and their latest values.
To add the table widget we need to select it from the widget library. Widgets are grouped into widget bundles. Each widget has a data source.
Let’s add your first widget:
  • Enter dashboard edit mode. Simply open the dashboard and click the "Edit mode" button found in the screen.
• Click the "Add widget" icon in the center of the screen (if this is your first widget on this dashboard)
• Select the "Tables" widget bundle
• Select the "Entities table" widget

• Click on the newly appeared data key input field. A list of available data keys will open. Select "temperature" data key.
  • Click the "Add" button in the bottom right corner of the widget to complete adding the widget.
• Click "Save" to apply changes.

Create New Customer -
Let’s create a customer with the title “My New Customer”.
  • Navigate to the Customers page. Click the "+" sign to add a customer.
• Input customer title and click "Add".

Assign the Device to Customer -

  • Open "Devices" page, then select your device to open its details.
• Click "Assign to customer" button.
• Select the customer to whom you want to assign the device, and then click "Assign".
• You have changed the owner of the device. In the "Customer" column, you can see the owners name of the device.

Assign the dashboard to customer -
  • Open "Dashboards" page. Click the "Assign dashboards" icon.
• Mark "My New Customer" and click "Assign".
• Navigate to the "Customers" page. Click "Manage customer dashboards" icon for "My New Customer".
• "My New Dashboard" is assigned to your customer.

Configure alarm rules -

We will use the alarm rule feature to raise the alarm when the temperature reading is greater than 25 degrees. For this purpose, we should edit the device profile and add a new alarm rule.
  • Go to the "Profiles" section, and click on the "Device profiles" page. Then click on the default device profile row to open its details.
• Select the "Alarm rules" tab and click "pencil" button to enter edit mode.
• Click "Add alarm rule" button.
• Specify alarm type and click the "+" icon to add alarm rule condition.

• Click the "Add key filter" button to specify a condition.
  • Select a key type, enter a key name, and select a value type. Then, click "Add" button in the "Filters" section.
• Select an operation and enter a threshold value. Click "Add" button in the lower right corner.
• Click "Save".
• Finally, click "Apply changes".

Create alarm -

Now, our alarm rule is active and we should send new telemetry on behalf of the device to trigger the alarm. Note that the temperature value should be 26 or higher to raise the alarm. Once we send a new temperature reading, we should immediately see a new alarm on our dashboard.
  • Notice that the new temperature telemetry causes a new active alarm.
  • You may acknowledge and clear the alarms.
  • When you receive a new alarm, you will receive a message in the notification center. You can view the message by clicking on the bell icon in the upper right corner.

Create New Asset Profile :-
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Profiles" section. Then go to the "Asset Profile" page.
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new Asset Profile".

Edit Asset Profile -
  • Clicking the asset profile row to open its details, then click the "pencil" icon in the upper right corner of the dialog.
• If necessary, change the asset profile name and any things. After editing, click the orange check mark to save all applied changes.

Delete Asset Profile -
  • Click on the “trash can” icon can opposite the asset profile name you want to delete.

Create New Assets :-
  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Entities" section. Then go to the "Asset" page.
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new asset".
• Enter the asset name. For example, "My New Asset". Click "Add" to add the asset.
• Your first asset has been added. As new asset are added, they will be added to the top of the table, since the table sort assets using the time of the creation by default.

Edit Asset -
  • Clicking the asset row to open its details, then click the "pencil" icon in the upper right corner of the dialog.
• If necessary, change the asset name and its profile. Also, you can input label and description. After editing, click the orange check mark to save all applied changes.

Delete Asset -
  • Click on the “trash can” icon can opposite the asset’s name you want to delete.
• Confirm deleting the asset in the dialog box.

  • Login to your Optima instance and navigate to the "Profiles" section. Then go to the "Device Profile" page.
  • Click on the "+" icon in the top right corner of the table and then select "Add new Device Profile".

By default, the Root Rule Chain processes all incoming messages and events for any device. However, the more different device types you have, the more complex your Root Rule Chain may become. Many platform users create their Root Rule Chain for the sole purpose of sending messages to specific rule chains depending on the device type.

• Enter the details. Click "Add" to add the device profile.
• Your first device profile has been added. As new device profile are added, they will be added to the top of the table, since the table sort assets using the time of the creation by default.

Transport Configuration :
The current version of the Optima platform supports the following transport types: Default, MQTT, CoAP

  • Clicking the device profile row to open its details, then click the "pencil" icon in the upper right corner of the dialog.
